# Copilot Instructions

- [x] Verify that the copilot-instructions.md file in the .github directory is created.
  Summary: Created `.github/copilot-instructions.md` at the workspace root.

- [x] Clarify Project Requirements
  Summary: Requirements confirmed from the user request and Stitch export: React, Vite, Tailwind CSS, local-first, future Vercel-ready, routed pages, accessibility, SEO, Australian English copy, and replacement imagery slots.

- [x] Scaffold the Project
  Summary: Scaffolded the Vite React app into the existing empty `Website` folder and installed required project dependencies.

- [x] Customize the Project
  Summary: Built the NewHope Abilities routed website with shared layout, Tailwind design tokens, page sections, content, SEO helpers, accessibility tools, local images, and Vercel rewrite support.

- [x] Install Required Extensions
  Summary: No VS Code extensions were specified by the project setup information.

- [x] Compile the Project
  Summary: `npm run lint` and `npm run build` complete successfully, and VS Code diagnostics report no errors.

- [x] Create and Run Task
  Summary: Created and ran the `NewHope Abilities: dev server` VS Code task.

- [x] Launch the Project
  Summary: Local dev server is running at `http://127.0.0.1:5173/`.

- [x] Ensure Documentation is Complete
  Summary: Updated `Website/README.md` and cleaned this instruction file of scaffold comments.

## Workspace Notes

- The app lives in `Website/`.
- The Stitch export remains unchanged in `stitch_newhope_abilities_digital_platform/` as reference material.
- Keep the project local-first until production deployment details are confirmed.
- Before deployment, confirm the production domain, contact-form provider, final legal copy, and whether the generated illustration set needs final art direction approval.
